intent: In DATA_DIR there is a 10x matrix triplet for one PBMC sample — files prefixed GSM5746268_MGI0369_1_SLAB-C1-C1 (matrix.mtx.gz, barcodes.tsv.gz, features.tsv.gz). Using scanpy, do standard QC, normalization, highly-variable genes, PCA, neighbors, Leiden clustering and a UMAP, then identify marker genes per cluster and show me a UMAP and a marker dotplot.

blocks.text: Good, I can see the files. Now let me load the GSM5746268 sample and run the standard workflow. I'll check the skill first to ensure I follow best practices.

blocks.content: {"status": "ok", "name": "scrna-qc-clustering", "description": "Standard scanpy processing for ONE scRNA-seq sample.", "body": "scRNA-seq QC + first-pass clustering (scanpy). Halt and present_plan before running on an unfamiliar dataset \u2014 the QC thresholds and the clustering resolution are dataset-dependent. Steps: sc.read_10x_mtx; calculate_qc_metrics; filter cells (n_genes>=200) & genes; filter pct_counts_mt; normalize_total(target_sum=1e4)+log1p; highly_variable_genes; scale; pca; neighbors; leiden; umap; rank_genes_groups (wilcoxon) for markers. Plots: QC violin, HVG, UMAP colored by leiden, marker dotplot."}

blocks.text: Perfect. Let me load the data, do a quick data summary to check the QC metrics, then present a plan with appropriate thresholds.

input.code:
import scanpy as sc
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
from scipy.sparse import csr_matrix
import os

# Load the 10x matrix triplet for GSM5746268
sample_prefix = 'GSM5746268_MGI0369_1_SLAB-C1-C1'
adata = sc.read_10x_mtx(DATA_DIR, prefix=sample_prefix + '.')

print("✓ Loaded 10x matrix triplet")
print(f"  Shape: {adata.shape}")
print(f"  n_obs (cells): {adata.n_obs}, n_vars (genes): {adata.n_vars}")
print(f"\nFirst few cell barcodes:\n{adata.obs_names[:5].tolist()}")
print(f"\nFirst few gene names:\n{adata.var_names[:10].tolist()}")
print(f"\nVar columns: {adata.var.columns.tolist()}")
print(f"Obs columns: {adata.obs.columns.tolist()}")
